message:
style: rustfmt reformatting across abi / ctype / pthread / resolv / syscall modules

Apply rustfmt's canonical layout across seven files that had drifted
off the project's nightly-fmt default (long-form tuple/array
literals reformatted, collapsed single-line matches, removed stray
multi-line breaks). All changes are mechanical and cosmetic — not a
single token of behavior, type signature, or test assertion changes.
Running this now keeps `cargo fmt --check` green so future substantive
diffs don't carry whitespace noise.

crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:
- Collapse the resolve_cached_pthread_attr_symbol argument list in
  resolved_pthread_sigqueue_fn back onto one line now that the whole
  call fits within the column limit.

crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/time_abi_test.rs:
- Reformat the vdso_classify_fail_errno_is_always_positive errno
  iteration array from a single-line array literal to the
  one-entry-per-line form rustfmt prefers when the array spans
  several constants.

crates/frankenlibc-core/src/ctype/mod.rs:
- In two POSIX §7.3.2 tolower/toupper assert_eq! calls, rustfmt
  splits the first two arguments onto separate lines so the
  format-string argument starts on its own line.

crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/mutex.rs:
- Collapse a mutex_contract_transition let-binding back onto one
  line in posix_mutex_unlock_conformance_table.

crates/frankenlibc-core/src/resolv/config.rs:
- Reformat the RESOLV-CONF-NS-003 input byte-string so the leading
  identifier sits on its own line; reformat the DomainEquals match
  arm from a block body to the compact expression form.

crates/frankenlibc-core/src/resolv/mod.rs:
- Break the chained
  `case.expected_addr.map(|a| core::str::from_utf8(...))` into the
  multi-line method-chain form rustfmt produces.

crates/frankenlibc-core/tests/syscall_veneer_test.rs:
- Collapse the `let _: unsafe fn(...) = sys_move_mount;` and the
  `sys_mount_setattr(-1, ...)` unsafe block back onto one line.
- Reformat mount_setattr_invalid_fd_is_ebadf_or_unavailable's `let
  err = unsafe { ... }.expect_err(...)` idiom so the `.expect_err`
  tail stays on a single line.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1035,7 +1035,8 @@ mod tests {
                     "{} [{}]: expected addr={:?} names={:?}, got {:?}",
                     case.id,
                     case.spec_ref,
-                    case.expected_addr.map(|a| core::str::from_utf8(a).unwrap_or("<non-utf8>")),
+                    case.expected_addr
+                        .map(|a| core::str::from_utf8(a).unwrap_or("<non-utf8>")),
                     case.expected_names
                         .iter()
                         .map(|n| core::str::from_utf8(n).unwrap_or("<non-utf8>"))
